FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Dec-15-2020 Case Transferred

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Proof Of Transfer To The County Of Marin
Off Calendar Dec-15-2020 Case Transferred

PETITION TO TRANSFER TO MARIN COUNTY
Off Calendar Oct-08-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa, Esq., counsel for Thomas Lucas, Conservator (appearing by video conference).

Mr. Siracusa advises the Court that the Conservatee is now resident in Marin County and as a matter of practice, the matter is properly transferred to the Marin County Superior Court for review and administration.
The Court takes the matter under submission with he stated intention to grant the petition. The Court sets hearing on the status of the transfer for January 12, 2021 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported; Bailiff: Deputy Johnson. (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Feb-04-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-16-299463 Conservatorship of Rosa Dettling and
PTR-16-299953 Rosa Dettling Revocable Trust.

Appearances: Andrew Schultz for and with Andrea Leung (Conservator of Person) and for Thomas Lucas (Conservator of Estate).
Jennifer Lefferts (Friend of Conservatee) in Propria Persona.

Ms. Lefferts stated that she is also representing the Conservatee's family. The Court informed Ms. Lefferts that she can represent anyone else. Mr. Schultz stated that the dog had been with the Conservatee her whole life, that the Conservatee was completely devoted to the dog, that the dog provided companionship and that the dog has since passed away. Mr. Schultz also stated that the Conservatee is in a facility in Marin County, that she has a house in San Francisco and that he will consider change of venue. Ms. Lefferts stated that she was not aware that she could be the conservator, that she was promised that she could continue to care for Ms. Dettling at a rate of $30.00 per hour and that she was told by the Family that the conservators are spending $23,000.00 a month. Ms. Lefferts also stated that she was very upset that she was not advised properly, that Mr. Lucas removed documents (power of attorney), that she requested a 1099 from Mr. Lucas and requested that she be paid $95.00 an hour for her services. The Court suggested that Ms. Lefferts get an Attorney, explained that the issues she raised are not before the Court and that anything she wants the Court to consider will have to be filed in the form of a petition. The Second Account and Report of Conservators, etc. and the Second Account and Report of Trustee, etc. are under submission.
Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.
